headline: Community Contribution | Wiki | openEuler
description: Learn how to contribute to openEuler through code, content, and community collaboration. Enhance your open source skills.
name: Open Source Contribution
How to Contribute to the openEuler Community
description: Step-by-step guide on how to contribute to the openEuler open source community, including submitting issues, PRs, and software packages.

step.name: Sign the Contributor License Agreement (CLA)
step.text: Sign the openEuler community Contributor License Agreement (CLA) before contributing to the community. Choose a CLA type based on whether you are contributing as an individual, an employee, or on behalf of a corporation.

step.name: Submit Issues or Pull Requests
step.text: Participate in projects you are interested in by submitting issues or PRs. You can quickly find or submit issues through QuickIssue. Issues and PRs are used for bug reporting and fixing, suggestion submission, task tracking, documentation feedback, and discussion.

step.name: Contribute a Software Package
step.text: Add a software package by forking the openeuler/community repository on Gitee, cloning it locally, and modifying the community repository to add the package to the appropriate SIG and OBS project.

mainEntity.name: What can I do to contribute to the openEuler community?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contributions to the openEuler community extend far beyond coding. You can submit issues or pull requests, contribute software packages, participate in SIG meetings, join mailing lists, and engage in technical exchange groups. Every effort, big or small, is welcomed and valued.

mainEntity.name: How do I submit an issue or PR to the openEuler community?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can quickly find or submit issues through QuickIssue at https://quickissue.openeuler.org/. Issues and PRs are commonly used for bug reporting and fixing, suggestion submission, task tracking, feedback on missing documentation or installation problems, and discussion.

mainEntity.name: How do I add a software package to the openEuler community?

acceptedAnswer.text: To add a software package, fork the openeuler/community repository on Gitee, clone it locally using git clone https://gitee.com/$username/community.git, then modify the repository to add your package to the appropriate SIG directory and OBS project.

mainEntity.name: Do I need to sign a CLA before contributing to openEuler?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you must sign the openEuler community Contributor License Agreement (CLA) before contributing. You can choose a CLA type based on whether you are contributing as an individual, an employee, or on behalf of a corporation. Sign at https://clasign.osinfra.cn/sign/6983225bdcbb19710248ccf0.
